message:
feat(db,cli): stage-then-swap archive restore and rollback-journal sidecar awareness

Two related safety improvements to SQLite database management:

## Archive Restore: Stage-Then-Swap

The previous archive restore extracted the snapshot directly into the
live database path, meaning a corrupt or truncated snapshot would
destroy the live database before the health check could catch it. The
new flow:

1. validate_archive_restore_targets() — verify parent dirs are real,
   target paths are sane (not symlinks to unexpected locations)
2. stage_archive_restore_snapshot() — extract snapshot to a tempdir
   adjacent to the target, run sqlite_file_is_healthy() on the staged
   copy BEFORE touching the live database
3. stage_archive_restore_storage_root() — extract storage repo to a
   separate adjacent tempdir
4. Only after both staging steps pass, rename (atomic on same fs) the
   staged files into the live paths

If the staged snapshot fails the health check, the live database and
storage repo are completely untouched — no backup needed, no rollback
needed. Adds test: archive_restore_state_keeps_live_state_when_archive_snapshot_is_invalid

## Rollback-Journal Sidecar Awareness

Extends the existing WAL/SHM sidecar monitoring to also track the
SQLite rollback-journal (-journal) file across all subsystems:

- pool.rs: MailboxSidecarState gains journal_exists/journal_bytes;
  inspect_mailbox_sidecar_state() probes the -journal file;
  sqlite_file_has_live_sidecars() and remove_sqlite_sidecars() now
  iterate SQLITE_RECOVERY_SIDECAR_SUFFIXES (includes -journal);
  recovery messaging updated throughout
- forensics.rs: ForensicPreSnapshot gains journal_bytes; capture adds
  -journal to family_paths for process holder and file lock scanning;
  forensic bundle captures journal artifacts; live_db_reference reports
  journal status
- mailbox_verdict.rs: probe_sidecar_state() now warns on rollback
  journal presence (journal-mode databases are suspect in WAL-mode
  deployments); describe_sidecar_state() provides human-readable
  sidecar detail strings; DurabilityFaultClass::StaleSidecars doc
  updated
- startup_checks.rs: attempt_probe_recovery() logs journal_bytes in
  pre-recovery snapshot

Adds tests across all affected crates for journal detection, forensic
reporting, sidecar state inspection, and probe warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2077,6 +2077,7 @@ fn attempt_probe_recovery(config: &Config) -> ProbeResult {
     tracing::info!(
         trigger = pre_snapshot.trigger,
         db_bytes = ?pre_snapshot.db_bytes,
+        journal_bytes = ?pre_snapshot.journal_bytes,
         wal_bytes = ?pre_snapshot.wal_bytes,
         holders = pre_snapshot.process_holders.len(),
         recovery_lock_active = pre_snapshot.recovery_lock_active,
